title: How The Military Feeds Hungry Marines, Army Soldiers, West Point Cadets, And More | Marathon
body: These chefs are the fuel for the front line. Those hot dishes and desserts raises morale more than anything. You get that home cooked meal, makes you think of home. You come in from nothing but days of MREs to a hot meal, puts a smile on your face. Then when they get out, they will land a job as a sous chef in an upscale restaurant. Great training. Thank you for your service chefs.
author: @RVAguy

title: How The Military Feeds Hungry Marines, Army Soldiers, West Point Cadets, And More | Marathon
body: honestly, being a prep cook is so much fun. you get to crack up with the other prep people, there's always something to do, it's a major work out, you learn to cook, plus you're providing a service for people and get to hear and see their enjoyment!! the amount of uncles and aunties i made being a prep cook will always out weight the burns and soreness i sustained from it lmao
author: @dnsoulx

title: How The Military Feeds Hungry Marines, Army Soldiers, West Point Cadets, And More | Marathon

title: How The Military Feeds Hungry Marines, Army Soldiers, West Point Cadets, And More | Marathon
body:
I just know the people you want to be friends with.
Supply, mechanic, chef, and Doc.

Logistics wins wars. Mechanics get you there. Doc keeps you healthy. And everyone marches on with Chef.
author: @SamlSchulze1104

title: How The Military Feeds Hungry Marines, Army Soldiers, West Point Cadets, And More | Marathon
body: I cooked in a military and civilian college for 15yrs..We had the salad bar, sandwich, grill, pizza/pasta, dessert, soup ect and we feed 1,200 for lunch and 1,500-1,800 for dinner. Dinner was 3hrs but everyone came in in the first 1hr 30mins. Pizza was 50-90 pizzas per meal and around 500 or more burgers. Loved cooking for our cadets.. Great video 🇺🇲 🇺🇲 🇺🇲
author: @andream1216

title: "Battle Hymn of the Republic" w/ the Mormon Tabernacle Choir LIVE from West Point | West Point Band
body: I'm Korean but I always wanna be an american. When I did my military service for my motherland, my battle buddies were you guys Americans and I'm always proud of them. God Bless Korea and America!
author: @jun5681

title: "Battle Hymn of the Republic" w/ the Mormon Tabernacle Choir LIVE from West Point | West Point Band
body:
The only time I saw my dad cry was to this song.

He was a Green Beret and was away when a young man in our church was killed by a land mine in Vietnam. My dad was asked to bring his body home. My dad was standing in the aisle of the church with the casket behind him. My grandpa was the pastor of the church. My family was seated in the balcony. He was standing there in his dress uniform larger than life itself. He tried but he couldn’t hold back the tears. I’m guessing he felt some guilt. My dad did four tours in Southeast Asia, cracked two vertebrae in his neck and fractured his skull in a parachuting accident and lived to see another day. The soldier was there for four months and didn’t survive. He also joined the Army because of my dad. I learned that day my dad was a human being. He was still my hero then and now. He’s been gone five years. I miss him but I find strength in knowing that I will one day see him again.

I couldn’t listen to the song without crying until he passed away. I know that when he passed that burden he carried had been lifted away by God’s mercy.
author: @Rick4345

title: "Battle Hymn of the Republic" w/ the Mormon Tabernacle Choir LIVE from West Point | West Point Band
body: My Dad was a Korean War combat vet.  When he passed 45 years ago the Church choir sang this song.    The Battle Hymn of the Republic always brings  proud tear to my eyes.
author: @Cha-y412

title: "Battle Hymn of the Republic" w/ the Mormon Tabernacle Choir LIVE from West Point | West Point Band
body: I bet when Julia Howe wrote this she never would have guessed it could be sung with so much passion and force and I think she would have liked it.

title: "Battle Hymn of the Republic" w/ the Mormon Tabernacle Choir LIVE from West Point | West Point Band

title: "Battle Hymn of the Republic" w/ the Mormon Tabernacle Choir LIVE from West Point | West Point Band
body:
I cry at our National Anthem and the Battle Hymm of the Republic, and I sob at
"In the beauty of the lilies Christ was born across the sea
With a glory in his bosom that transfigures you and me
As he died to make men holy let us die to make men free
His truth is marching on."
author: @22lyric

title: "Battle Hymn of the Republic" w/ the Mormon Tabernacle Choir LIVE from West Point | West Point Band
body: While I can’t claim to be a Christian, I can align myself with the value of devoting ones life to the freedom of another that so clearly comes out of these lyrics. This is a beautiful presentation of what we can call a “transversal” value
author: @ronaldstokes-walters6647

title: "Battle Hymn of the Republic" w/ the Mormon Tabernacle Choir LIVE from West Point | West Point Band
body:
Mine eyes have seen the glory of the coming of the Lord
He is trampling out the vintage where the grapes of wrath are stored,
He has loosed the fateful lightening of His terrible swift sword
His truth is marching on.
Glory! Glory! Hallelujah!
Glory! Glory! Hallelujah!
Glory! Glory! Hallelujah!
His truth is marching on.
I have seen Him in the watch-fires of a hundred circling camps
They have builded Him an altar in the evening dews and damps
I can read His righteous sentence by the dim and flaring lamps
His day is marching on.
Glory! Glory! Hallelujah!
Glory! Glory! Hallelujah!
Glory! Glory! Hallelujah!
His truth is marching on.
I have read a fiery gospel writ in burnish'd rows of steel,
As ye deal with my condemners, So with you my grace shall deal;
Let the Hero, born of woman, crush the serpent with his heel
Since God is marching on.
Glory! Glory! Hallelujah!
Glory! Glory! Hallelujah!
Glory! Glory! Hallelujah!
His truth is marching on.
He has sounded form the trumpet that shall never call retreat
He is sifting out the hearts of men before His judgment-seat
Oh, be swift, my soul, to answer Him! be jubilant, my feet!
Our God is marching on.
Glory! Glory! Hallelujah!
Glory! Glory! Hallelujah!
Glory! Glory! Hallelujah!
His truth is marching on.
Ln the beauty of the lillies Christ was born across the sea,
With a glory in His bosom that transfigures you and me:
As He died to make men holy, let us die to make men free,
While God is marching on.
Glory! Glory! Hallelujah!
Glory! Glory! Hallelujah!
Glory! Glory! Hallelujah!
His truth is marching on.
author: @baconfrancis9889

title: "Battle Hymn of the Republic" w/ the Mormon Tabernacle Choir LIVE from West Point | West Point Band

This makes me so very proud to be an American!

author: @kareny5438

title: What lunch looks like for #cadets at West Point. #military #WestPoint #lunch
body: I got to go visit West Point when my brother graduated. They served us dinner with all the graduating seniors, but the civilians were seated together away from the cadets and it was indeed the most "proper" etiquette meal I've ever been a part of. Yes the guy is shown just dropping the silverware on the plate, but that is his job. Someone else is right behind him setting it all up precisely and consistently.
author: @halecj1

title: What lunch looks like for #cadets at West Point. #military #WestPoint #lunch
body: 25 minutes is an insane amount of time. No wonder army officers think they’re better than everyone, they’ve been treated like it since day one.
author: @TeufelHunden927

title: What lunch looks like for #cadets at West Point. #military #WestPoint #lunch
body: I always had respect for the cooks and civilians defac staff when I was enlisted. Hats off to these hard working people.
author: @childrey14

title: What lunch looks like for #cadets at West Point. #military #WestPoint #lunch
body: My son got accepted and only stayed one year. Said was just not for him. I was upset but he had plan B. Got a scholarship to an AF rotc program and now a Captain in AF.
author: @sad754yt

title: What lunch looks like for #cadets at West Point. #military #WestPoint #lunch
body: I have the amazing opportunity to compete at Westpoint for debate several times between 2007 and 2011. Thankfully my experience eating there was Sunday mornings after the full competition was done. They had an amazing buffet spread for everybody but the cadets did the serving, it is one of the most beautiful places I’ve ever had the opportunity to see, it looks like Hogwarts! And the view from the dining area is breathtaking!
author: @EvolvementEras

title: What lunch looks like for #cadets at West Point. #military #WestPoint #lunch
body: 8 minutes. That was for me to rush down 2 stairs, order my lunch and finish it, when I was in high school.

title: What New Army Cadets Go Through During The First Six Weeks At West Point | Boot Camp
body: To those that are unaware: This is Officer training. THIS IS NOT boot camp for enlisted. And while there might be many similarities, they are quite different. For one, they are much kinder to cadets than they are privates.
author: @theflychiguy

title: What New Army Cadets Go Through During The First Six Weeks At West Point | Boot Camp
body: Don’t compare bootcamp with the trainings these cadets do, their instructors are not drill sergeants, but rather upper class men/ officers. The young officers are there to study academics and not in a hurry to be sent to the frontline. They still have to endure 4 more years of academic pressure plus military pressure so they aren’t taking it easy.
author: @jimchen3762

title: What New Army Cadets Go Through During The First Six Weeks At West Point | Boot Camp
body: Being yelled at and having the self discipline to stay calm in high intensity situations is masterism to me. masterism is a word I made up.
author: @SpiritualBabe101

title: What New Army Cadets Go Through During The First Six Weeks At West Point | Boot Camp
body: The young men and women that attend the service academies are among our country’s finest. They have a rare combination of academic prowess, physical fitness, discipline, and character. In addition, they chose to serve. I know several West Point graduates who I met after their military service was over. They were all high achieving people of great character long after their military service was over.
author: @emilgeorge35

title: What New Army Cadets Go Through During The First Six Weeks At West Point | Boot Camp
body: Yo that’s my buddy Ryan at 7:55. He was a senior last year and I was a sophomore. He’s the most genuine guy ever. He studied hard everyday and has an amazing, loving girlfriend who is super supportive of him. I’m glad to see he’s doing well!
author: @christianearle5605

title: What New Army Cadets Go Through During The First Six Weeks At West Point | Boot Camp
body: I served as an enlisted and climbed the ranks to non commissioned officer and it's great to see what the officers I served with had to endure before becoming an officer. NCO's may be the backbone of the Army because of our experiences but good officers make the call that we had to follow. I was fortunate to have served with a lot of great officers.

title: What New Army Cadets Go Through During The First Six Weeks At West Point | Boot Camp

title: What New Navy Plebes Go Through On Their First Day At Annapolis | Boot Camp | Business Insider
body:
As someone mentioned, you may need to go to Naval Academy Prep before heading off the the actual academy. It's a good chance to polish your skills before you arrive for plebe summer and your plebe year (freshman). The academy is a very challenging place and not everyone makes it thru.
Joining the Navy was one of my best decisions. Sometimes I was happy as heck to be there, other times I wanted out. There are a lot of ups and downs but the key is to stay with it and listen to what you are told.
I did go to college and I did get an education. But the Navy did a better job at preparing me for real life.
author: @13bcoffee

title: What Day One As A Marine, Army Soldier, And Border Patrol Officer Is Really Like | BI Marathon
body: Did army basic back in 2010. Idk how many pushups/planks I did for freaking smirking lol. the yelling was always funny to me. I grew up in Puerto Rico and the yelling was normal. Honestly those were probably the best years of my life. Met so many good ppl I'm proud to call my battles. God bless you all
author: @Deathdealer89

title: What Day One As A Marine, Army Soldier, And Border Patrol Officer Is Really Like | BI Marathon
body:
As a Veteran, I learned a number of things about serving this great Nation.
You conform to the military. The military does not conform to you, the individual.
Boot camp strips you down to the basics. It then builds you back to be part of the military.
author: @TelemarketerTroll

title: What Day One As A Marine, Army Soldier, And Border Patrol Officer Is Really Like | BI Marathon
body:
Retired Air Force 1990 - 2012
I hated Basic, but I wouldn't change being in for anything in the world.
author: @bigsarge8795

title: What Day One As A Marine, Army Soldier, And Border Patrol Officer Is Really Like | BI Marathon
body: My brother joined the Navy while I was distant for a bit from the family. He's sounded so proud of himself I love him so much
author: @KellyLove-u9w

title: What Day One As A Marine, Army Soldier, And Border Patrol Officer Is Really Like | BI Marathon
body: I graduated from boot camp at Ft. Sill, Ok a little over 20 years ago. They put us 100 deep in a cattle car with no seats and no air conditioning. When we got off some of toughest dudes I had ever seen were immediately in our faces trying to wear us down. As a young 18 year old I had no idea what I had signed up for until that moment. My time in the Army was some of the best in my life and it made me into who I am today. Wouldn’t trade it for nothing
author: @johnhessick6351

title: What Day One As A Marine, Army Soldier, And Border Patrol Officer Is Really Like | BI Marathon
body: I was definitely spoiled when I went through Marine boot camp back in 2014. None of my drill instructors were fat bodies, all were squared away with perfect uniforms at all times. I never saw them eat, drink, laugh, or smile. They were like robots filled with rage and i was treated like a piece of trash until the day I earned the title and became something more.
author: @BigMackM27

title: What Day One As A Marine, Army Soldier, And Border Patrol Officer Is Really Like | BI Marathon
body: I just had to click on this. Now I will probably be dreaming tonight that I’m back in basic. Happens about once a year. That was 50 years ago. Sometimes just don’t go away. I didn’t enjoy a minute of it at the time but I’m sure glad I did it.
author: @rickpowers4356

title: What Day One As A Marine, Army Soldier, And Border Patrol Officer Is Really Like | BI Marathon
body: RTC Great Lakes, 1976 Co 941. There was no screaming and yelling the entire 13 weeks, we all did just fine, the Navy was great back then.
author: @bluejacket9774

title: What Day One As A Marine, Army Soldier, And Border Patrol Officer Is Really Like | BI Marathon
body: Advice I got from someone who served was to get as much sleep as possible whilst on the bus, screw getting to know the person sitting next to you try and get whatever sleep you can, cause once you're off that bus you won't be getting sleep or much sleep at all during the time you arrive(BTW THIS IS MOSTLY FOR THOSE WHO WILL ARRIVE AT NIGHT TIME). He told me when he arrived there at the base camp location at close to 9:30pm it took them to 3:00 am to get into bed and guess what happened? they were woken up at 0500 2 hours after falling asleep and he straight up said it was hell.
author: @Titan.Fox56

title: What Day One As A Marine, Army Soldier, And Border Patrol Officer Is Really Like | BI Marathon
body: I know this is completely off subject, but does no one else see the absolutely gorgeous, candy striped lawns at some of these military institutions? Those lines! From one professional to another, brother landscaper, you have great skill and my utmost respect. Your skills on that mower rivals that of an artist with a passion and a paint brush! Respect.
author: @chrisnash1290

title: What Day One As A Marine, Army Soldier, And Border Patrol Officer Is Really Like | BI Marathon
body: That guy at 6:30 was me when i joined in 2019. "Im broke man idk what to do" lmaooo
author: @seancrosby5269

title: What Day One As A Marine, Army Soldier, And Border Patrol Officer Is Really Like | BI Marathon
body: Don't be fooled; If you're not easily annoyed (which you learn to not be,) boot camp is actually a cake-walk. Just go through the motions, it's not gonna hurt you at all. It's even fun to some of us.
author: @NathanXTZ

title: What Day One As A Marine, Army Soldier, And Border Patrol Officer Is Really Like | BI Marathon
body: I have ALWAYS respected our military men/woman but watching this makes me respect them 10 times more!   They are doing this and going through this because they chose to do so to serve and protect this great country.
author: @jakereal3604

title: What Day One As A Marine, Army Soldier, And Border Patrol Officer Is Really Like | BI Marathon
body: Man, I went to basic for the Army in '97 and never got a snack 😥 Shark attack was intense. There was nothing better than Senior Drill Sergeant getting on the bus and welcoming us and then telling us we have 3 seconds to get off his bus.
author: @JasonT850
